title: Clubfoot & Limb Development: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Clubfoot & Limb Development
Identify key diagnostic features of Clubfoot & Limb Development
Apply clinical reasoning to Clubfoot & Limb Development management
Recognize common pitfalls in Clubfoot & Limb Development

sections.title: Overview of Clubfoot & Limb Development
sections.content:
**Clubfoot & Limb Development**

Limb bud development, AER and ZPA signaling, clubfoot (talipes equinovarus), Ponseti method

**Key Points:**
• Limb buds appear week 4, upper before lower
• AER (apical ectodermal ridge): FGFs → proximal-distal growth
• ZPA (zone of polarizing activity): SHH → anterior-posterior patterning

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Limb buds appear week 4, upper before lower
• AER (apical ectodermal ridge): FGFs → proximal-distal growth

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• ZPA (zone of polarizing activity): SHH → anterior-posterior patterning
• Clubfoot (talipes equinovarus): hindfoot equinus + varus, forefoot adductus + supination
• Ponseti method: serial casting (gold standard) → Achilles tenotomy → brace; Surgery if refractory

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Clubfoot & Limb Development:**

• ZPA (zone of polarizing activity): SHH → anterior-posterior patterning
• Clubfoot (talipes equinovarus): hindfoot equinus + varus, forefoot adductus + supination
• Ponseti method: serial casting (gold standard) → Achilles tenotomy → brace; Surgery if refractory
